rationale: The citing paper uses Sethuraman et al. (2011) in its core interpretation of results, not merely in a generic literature list. It explicitly compares its estimated average follower elasticity (.10) to the cited paper's meta-analytic short-term advertising elasticity (.12) to position influencer marketing against traditional advertising evidence. This is a meaningful benchmarking role, even though the citing paper does not directly reuse or extend the cited paper's methodology.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Sethuraman et al. (2011) as an empirical benchmark for effect size, comparing FEI estimates to established advertising elasticity generalizations. This comparison helps frame the contribution and interpret whether influencer marketing effectiveness is similar to traditional advertising.
